title: How to get a police certificate – Vietnam - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Vietnam
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Judicial Certificate #2 or Criminal Record Certificate #2 ( Phieu Ly Lich Tu Phap So 2 )

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.

### If you are a Vietnamese national
- apply in person or by mail to the Department of Justice office located in the district where your permanent address is registered
- apply online through the Public Service Portal for your area The Public Service Portal website will take the following format: https://dichvucong.PROVINCENAME.gov.vn (example: http://dichvucong.angiang.gov.vn ).
- The Public Service Portal website will take the following format: https://dichvucong.PROVINCENAME.gov.vn (example: http://dichvucong.angiang.gov.vn ).

#### If you live in Hanoi
If you apply online, you must submit your application via the Online Judicial Certification Registration Service of Department of Justice of Hanoi City ( Dich vu dang ky cap phieu ly lich tu phap truc Tuyen – So Tu Phap Thanh pho Ha Noi ).
If your permanent residence records have been annulled pursuant Article 29, the Residence Law 2020 (available in Vietnamese only), you must:
- get a confirmation letter from the district police in your last place of permanent residence which states that your permanent residence registration was deleted
- use the confirmation letter to apply in person or by mail to the National Center for Judicial Records ( Trung Tam Ly Lich Tu Phap Quoc Gia ) (check Contact Information for the complete address).

### If you are not a Vietnamese national

#### If you live in Hanoi, Vietnam
Apply through the Online Judicial Certification Registration Service of Department of Justice of Hanoi City ( Dich vu dang ky cap phieu ly lich tu phap truc Tuyen – So Tu Phap Thanh pho Ha Noi ).

#### If you live in a city other than Hanoi, Vietnam
- apply in person or by mail to the Department of Justice office located in the district where your permanent address is registered
- apply online through the Public Service Portal for your area The Public Service Portal website will take the following format: https://dichvucong.PROVINCENAME.gov.vn (example: http://dichvucong.angiang.gov.vn ).
- The Public Service Portal website will take the following format: https://dichvucong.PROVINCENAME.gov.vn (example: http://dichvucong.angiang.gov.vn ).

#### If you no longer live in Vietnam
You can apply in person or by mail to the National Center for Judicial Records ( Trung Tam Ly Lich Tu Phap Quoc Gia ) (check Contact Information for the complete address).
National Center of Criminal Records
Address: 9 Tran Vi Street, Mai Dinh ward, Cau Giay District, Hanoi.
Telephone: +84 24 6273 9492
Email: ttlltpqg@moj.gov.vn
